evals.prompt: I have an existing Dashfy project. Add the GitHub extension and place a RepoBadge for vercel/next.js and a Status widget on a new 4x3 dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Runs `dashfy add @getdashfy/github`, then adds widget blocks with full grid coordinates to dashfy.config.yml and reminds the user to set GITHUB_TOKEN.
expectations: Uses `dashfy add @getdashfy/github` (or bare `github`) instead of manually editing App.tsx / the server / .env
Adds widget blocks with extension, widget, x, y, columns, and rows keys (not just props)
Keeps widgets inside the 4x3 grid (x + columns <= 4, y + rows <= 3) with no overlap
Only references real GitHub widget exports (e.g. RepoBadge, Status) and passes repository as a prop
Notes that GITHUB_TOKEN must be set in .env (seeded as an empty placeholder by add)
Suggests verifying with `dashfy doctor`

evals.prompt: Author a new Dashfy extension registry item for a Weather extension that has CurrentConditions and Forecast widgets, polls an API using a WEATHER_API_KEY, and seeds a CurrentConditions starter for Lisbon.
evals.expected_output: Adds a `dashfy` field to the extension's package.json with id, title, widgets, a poll client, envVars, and starter, then builds and validates the registry.
expectations: Authors the metadata as the `dashfy` field in the extension's package.json (not hand-written item JSON)
Sets id, title, and widgets[] matching real exports (CurrentConditions, Forecast)
Defines client with import, factory, mode: 'poll', and options reading process.env.WEATHER_API_KEY
Declares envVars: ['WEATHER_API_KEY'] so add seeds it
Adds a starter block { widget: 'CurrentConditions', city: 'Lisbon' } without manual grid coordinates
Runs `dashfy registry build` then `dashfy registry validate`

evals.prompt: Lay out a system-monitor dashboard: CPU, memory, and disk gauges across the top row, and a CPU usage line chart spanning the full width below. The system extension is already installed.
evals.expected_output: A dashfy.config.yml dashboard with a correct non-overlapping grid using real system widget names.
expectations: Edits dashfy.config.yml (does not re-run add for an already-installed extension, or treats re-add as a no-op)
Places the three gauges in the top row at x: 0, 1, 2 with columns: 1, rows: 1 (no overlap)
Makes the line chart span the full width (e.g. x: 0, y: 1, columns: 4) within the grid bounds
Uses real system widget exports (e.g. CpuUsageGauge, MemoryUsageGauge, DiskUsageGauge, CpuUsageLine)
Every widget block includes extension, widget, x, y, columns, rows

evals.prompt: I want to install an extension from a community registry I haven't configured yet: @acme/incidents. Set it up and add it.
evals.expected_output: Configures the @acme registry (via discovery index or explicit URL) then adds the extension; does not guess the registry URL.
expectations: Classifies @acme/incidents as a namespace address that must be configured
Configures the registry via `dashfy registry add @acme` (discovery index) or an explicit @acme=URL pair, or relies on add's auto-config prompt
Does not invent or hardcode a registry URL without basis
Runs `dashfy add @acme/incidents` after the namespace is configured
Mentions verifying with `dashfy doctor` and setting any required env vars
